title: Benicio Del Toro Joins Ben Stiller in Apple TV Comedy ‘Protective Custody’
description:
Benicio Del Toro is set for a lead role opposite Ben Stiller in the upcoming Apple TV comedy series “Protective Custody,” Variety has learned. 

	Th
body:
Benicio Del Toro is set for a lead role opposite Ben Stiller in the upcoming Apple TV comedy series “Protective Custody,” Variety has learned. 

	The show was originally announced in May with Stiller attached to star and executive produce. It marks the first lead series comedy role of Del Toro’s career as well as a reunion for him and Stiller. The two previously collaborated on the Showtime limited series “Escape at Dannemora,” which Stiller directed and in which Del Toro starred. Both received Emmy nominations for their work on the show. 
	

	The logline for “Protective Custody” states that the show “will follow a disgraced financier (Stiller) accused of massive fraud who is thrown into protective custody, where he must navigate prison politics, salvage his reputation, and confront the consequences of his actions while awaiting trial.” 


	Exact details on Del Toro’s character are being kept under wraps. 

	Del Toro is one of the most celebrated actors of his generation. He is a three-time Oscar nominee, winning the award for best supporting actor for “Traffic” in 2001, for which he also won a Golden Globe. He has also been nominated for his roles in “21 Grams” and “One Battle After Another,” the latter of which also netted him a Golden Globe nom. His other notable film roles include “Sicario” and “Sicario: Day of the Soldado,” Wes Anderson’s “The French Dispatch” and “The Phoenician Scheme,” and “Fear and Loathing in Las Vegas.” 

	He is repped by LBI Entertainment, Range Media Partners, and Hansen Jacobson. 

	Mike Judge is co-writing “Protective Custody” along with Steve Hely and Dave King. All three are executive producers and co-showrunners, with Judge also attached to direct. Stiller will executive produce in addition to starring via Red Hour. John Lesher of Red Hour also executive produces, as does Ben Silverman, Howard Owens, and Rodney Ferrell of Propagate Content. Michael Rotenberg and Oly Obst executive produce for 3 Arts Entertainment. Nanette Burstein will executive produce as well.

schema.headline: Benicio Del Toro Joins Ben Stiller in Apple TV Comedy ‘Protective Custody’

title: Julie Andrews Gets Disney+ Documentary From ‘Martha’ Director R.J. Cutler
description: Disney+ will launch a documentary about star Julie Andrews from director and producer R.J. Cutler (“Billie Eilish: The World’s A Little Blurry,” “Ma
body:
Disney+ will launch a documentary about star Julie Andrews from director and producer R.J. Cutler (“Billie Eilish: The World’s A Little Blurry,” “Martha”) next year. 

	Per the description for the project, “This definitive portrait offers an intimate and revealing look at Andrews’ extraordinary journey, from her early days in British vaudeville to her meteoric rise as a global superstar. Marked by resilience, heartbreak, reinvention and enduring grace, her story goes far beyond the spotlight.”
	

	The documentary about the star of iconic films including “Mary Poppins,” “The Sound of Music” and “The Princess Diaries” will include “rare, never-before-seen archival footage and candid new interviews.”


	An official title and 2027 release date will be announced at a later date.

	The Andrews documentary is produced by Cutler alongside Trevor Smith, Elise Pearlstein, Jane Cha Cutler and Jonathan Ruane. Mark Blatty is executive producer. The project hails from Sony Pictures Television’s This Machine.

	“Julie Andrews is not simply a beloved performer,” Cutler said. “She is a cultural touchstone, a figure woven into the fabric of how generations of people understand joy, resilience and grace. Most people don’t know the extraordinary challenges she’s had to overcome throughout her life, all of which we delve into in this film. To sit with Julie, to be allowed into her inner world, to watch her reflect on a life that has shaped so many other lives was an experience I will forever cherish. She gave us her full self, without armor, without pretense. And what we found was someone even more extraordinary than the icon. I am humbled by her talent, in awe of her strength and honored by her trust.”

schema.headline: Julie Andrews Gets Disney+ Documentary From ‘Martha’ Director R.J. Cutler

title: Kenneth Branagh to Star in Paramount+ Drama ‘Laird’ Set in Scottish Highlands
description:
Paramount+ has picked up the drama series “Laird” with Kenneth Branagh attached in the lead role. 

	The series hails from Jez Butterworth and John-
body:
Paramount+ has picked up the drama series “Laird” with Kenneth Branagh attached in the lead role. 

	The series hails from Jez Butterworth and John-Henry Butterworth, the co-creators of fellow Paramount+ series “The Agency.” Jez is also the co-creator and showrunner of Paramount+’s “Mobland.” Georgia Lee is also a co-creator on the show. 

	The logline for the show describes it as “an epic family saga in which a proud Scot defies a shadowy American billionaire hellbent on buying up the glens. Amidst family feuds, buried secrets and star-crossed romances, the Laird of Castle Drummoch (Branagh) remains determined to protect his ancestral lands from oligarchical land grabbing.” 
	

	Both the Butterworths are executive producers on “Laird,” with Branagh also executive producing in addition to starring. David Glasser, Joe Wright, Ron Burkle, David Hutkin, Bob Yari, Charles Finch, and Keith Cox also executive produce, with Wright also attached to direct the pilot. Paramount Television Studios and 101 Studios produce. 


	“At its heart, Laird is about one man standing his ground against unchecked wealth and power,” said Jane Wiseman, head of Originals for Paramount+. “There’s something universally resonant about watching someone fight to protect what matters most, and with Kenneth Branagh leading the series, and an equally remarkable creative team, we can’t wait for audiences to root for the Laird of Castle Drummoch”

	Branagh is primarily known for his roles in films, netting eight Oscar nominations throughout his career. He won the award for best original screenplay in 2022 for “Belfast,” which he also directed. His acting credits include hits like “Dunkirk,” “Tenet,” and “Oppenheimer” (all with Christopher Nolan) as well as films like “My Week with Marilyn” and multiple films as legendary detective Hercule Poirot. 
	

	He is also a four-time Emmy nominee, winning for outstanding lead actor in a miniseries or movie for “Conspiracy.” He was also nominated for his work in “Shackleton,” “Warm Springs,” and “Wallander.” 

	He is repped by WME, Berwick & Kovacik and Gochman Law Group. 

	“Jez, John-Henry, and Georgia have imagined a gripping drama that explores how far one family will go to protect their land and legacy,” said Matt Thunell, President, Paramount Television Studios. “With Kenneth Branagh starring and Joe Wright directing, this is a singular creative team, and we are thrilled to once again partner with 101 Studios to bring this series to Paramount+.”

	Paramount and 101 have a number of projects together at present, including Taylor Sheridan’s expansive slate at the streamer, “The Agency” and “Mobland.” 

	“Working with Jez and John Henry has been such an incredible experience these last few years. I couldn’t be more thrilled to take another epic journey with them,” said Glasser, CEO of 101 Studios.

schema.headline: Kenneth Branagh to Star in Paramount+ Drama ‘Laird’ Set in Scottish Highlands

title: Glen Powell, Richard Linklater Teaming Up for Lou Gehrig Biopic, Lorne Michaels Producing
description: Richard Linklater and Glen Powell are re-teaming to take on the Pride of the Yankees. The pair, who previously collaborated on “Hit Man” and “Everyb
body:
Richard Linklater and Glen Powell are re-teaming to take on the Pride of the Yankees. The pair, who previously collaborated on “Hit Man” and “Everybody Wants Some,” will join forces on an untitled Lou Gehrig film for Universal Pictures. 

	Gehrig is a Hall of Famer whose durability and endurance on the field (he played for a then-record 2,130 consecutive games) earned him the nickname “the Iron Horse.” A legendary slugger, his career was cut short when he was diagnosed with amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS). His farewell address to a sold-out crowd at Yankees stadium in 1939, in which he declared himself “the luckiest man on the face of the Earth,” has been dubbed “baseball’s Gettysburg address.” Gehrig died in 1941 at age 37. His story was previously dramatized in 1942’s “The Pride of the Yankees” with Gary Cooper playing the sports icon. 
	

	Lorne Michaels of “SNL” fame will produce the film with Erin David under his company Broadway Video’s first-look deal with Universal. Powell will produce alongside Dan Cohen for his shingle, Barnstorm, which also has a first-look deal with the studio. Linklater is in talks to produce through Detour Filmproduction.


	Simon Rich, an “SNL” vet, will write the script. Rich recently wrote “Artificial,” a look at OpenAI CEO Sam Altman that Luca Guadagnino directs. It generated headlines after Amazon MGM Studios dropped the film, which was later picked up by Neon. 

	Powell’s credits include “Twisters” and “The Running Man.” He will next appear in J.J. Abrams’s “The Great Beyond.” Linklater recently directed “Blue Moon,” which earned an Oscar nod for its script and for Ethan Hawke’s lead performance, as well as “Nouvelle Vague,” the story of the making of “Breathless.” 
	

	Powell is represented by CAA, The Initiative Group and Johnson Shapiro Slewett & Kole. Linklater is repped by CAA and Cinetic Media. Rich is represented by CAA and Eastman and Eastman.

	Deadline broke the news about the Gehrig biopic.

schema.headline: Glen Powell, Richard Linklater Teaming Up for Lou Gehrig Biopic, Lorne Michaels Producing

title: Victoria Monét Announces New Album ‘Frequency of Love,’ Releases New Single ‘Reach Out’
description:
Victoria Monét has announced her sophomore album “Frequency of Love,” releasing on October 2 via Lovett Music/RCA Records.

	To coincide with the pr
body:
Victoria Monét has announced her sophomore album “Frequency of Love,” releasing on October 2 via Lovett Music/RCA Records.

	To coincide with the project reveal, the R&B singer has dropped her new single “Reach Out,” produced by Kaytranada and D’Mile, as well as its video, directed by Dave Meyers and featuring choreography by frequent collaborator Sean Bankhead.



	Monét began teasing this new era earlier this year, releasing the first taste of what fans can expect with the sultry track “Let Me.” That single continued to reach No. 1 on the Billboard Adult R&B Airplay chart last month. Little is known otherwise about the album at large, but producer Camper revealed to Variety earlier this year that he would be serving as executive producer on the project.
	

	As she gears up for the release of “Frequency of Love,” Monét is currently wrapping up her supporting slot for Bruno Mars on the European and UK leg of “The Romantic Tour.” She joined the trek in mid-June and performed in cities including Paris, Amsterdam, Berlin, Madrid and Milan. She currently has three more dates scheduled for London’s Wembley Stadium Connected by EE for July 24, 25 and 28.

	A decorated singer and songwriter with credits for Ariana Grande and Chloe x Halle, Monét rose to mainstream prominence in 2023 following the release of her debut album “Jaguar II.” That record spawned the smash single “On My Mama” and paved the way for Monét to secure seven Grammy nominations. At the ceremony in 2024, she won three of her seven nominations, including best R&B album, best engineered album, non-classical and the coveted best new artist.

schema.headline: Victoria Monét Announces New Album ‘Frequency of Love,’ Releases New Single ‘Reach Out’

title: Casey Affleck’s Gothic Mystery ‘Company’ to Star Nick Nolte, Adelaide Clemens, Ben Mendelsohn and Scoot McNairy (EXCLUSIVE)
description:
Casey Affleck has directed his third feature and first since 2019’s “Light of My Life.”

	“Company,” which wrapped earlier this year in Whistler, Ca
body:
Casey Affleck has directed his third feature and first since 2019’s “Light of My Life.”

	“Company,” which wrapped earlier this year in Whistler, Canada, is a gothic mystery starring Nick Nolte (“Cape Fear,” “Die My Love”), Adelaide Clemens (“The Great Gatsby”), Ben Mendelsohn (“The Dark Knight Rises,” “The King”), Scoot McNairy (“A Complete Unknown,” “Speak No Evil”), Emily Alyn Lind (“We Were Liars,” “Speed-the-Plow”) and Caylee Cowan (“Sunrise in Heaven”).
	

	Written and directed by Affleck, a best actor Oscar winner for “Manchester by the Sea,” “Company” is a co-production between Image Nation Studios, Rabbits Black, Spooky Pictures, Artists Equity, Arcana Studio and Artemis Pictures. AGC and Spooky Pictures are on board to handle international sales, with Gersh, Cinetic and Range Select handling North America.


	Set on a winter night in 1975, the film sees a mysterious woman named Evelyn arrive uninvited at a holiday gathering and tell the story of Tom, an aging hermit who rescues a stranded actress during a Colorado blizzard more than 20 years earlier. To pass the night, the actress recounts an older tale of a farming couple who take in a troubled young drifter as a series of grisly murders spreads fear across the frontier. Per the synopsis, “As the stories intertwine across generations, ‘Company’ becomes a meditation on grief, vengeance, forgiveness and the quiet acts of compassion that can heal even the deepest wounds.”

	Steven Schneider (“The Long Walk,” “Strange Darling,” “Late Night With the Devil”) and Roy Lee (“Weapons,” “Barbarian,” “It”) are producing for Spooky Pictures, alongside Cowan, Dan Lawler, Affleck, Sean Patrick O’Reilly, K. Asher Levin, Enzo Marc and Anthony Katagas. Executive producers include Ben Ross (“Watcher,” “Late Night with the Devil”) for Image Nation Studios; Rami Yasin (“Hokum”) for Spooky Pictures; Ronnie Exley and Jeremy Ross for Rabbits Black; Ron McLeod, Dr. Terence Chan and Bryan Meng; along with Maria Breese for 3:33 Creative Productions and Jonathan Miller. Ron Hansen (“The Assassination of Jesse James by the Coward Robert Ford”) is co-writer.
	



	

					Casey Affleck
									Alexia Barroso
	



	“Casey is a visionary filmmaker who has once again brought his remarkable atmospheric style, emotional realism and psychological depth to create a quietly haunting gothic mystery,” Image Nation Studios’ CEO Ben Ross said. “It’s a privilege to be part of his latest work and to have such an esteemed lineup of talent and partners joining us for this journey.”

	Spooky Pictures co-founders Schneider and Lee added: “With ‘Company,’ Casey has crafted an intimate gothic mystery about the complex ties that bind us across generations, offering a powerful confrontation with how the past continues to ripple into the present. The film combines striking cinematic landscapes with emotionally powerful performances to create an experience for audiences that will be both quietly unsettling and profoundly human.”

	Artists Equity’s president of film & TV Amy Baer said: “’Company’ is a striking showcase of Casey’s vision as both a writer and director. It’s a haunting, emotionally resonant story with a strong cast behind it. We’re excited to partner with this team and can’t wait to share the film with the world.”

	Affleck is repped by Range Media Partners; Nolte by CAA and Tavistock Wood Management; Clemens by Gersh and Untitled Entertainment; Mendelsohn by WME; McNairy by UTA; Cowan by LBI Entertainment; and Alyn Lind by Brillstein and CAA.

schema.headline: Casey Affleck’s Gothic Mystery ‘Company’ to Star Nick Nolte, Adelaide Clemens, Ben Mendelsohn and Scoot McNairy (EXCLUSIVE)

title: ‘Big Bang Theory’ Spinoff ‘Stuart Fails to Save the Universe’ Is a Playful, Creative Romp: TV Review
description: In a sea of perfunctory prequels and sequels, you have to hand it to a spinoff that actually comes up with a creative angle. Such is the case with “
body:
In a sea of perfunctory prequels and sequels, you have to hand it to a spinoff that actually comes up with a creative angle. Such is the case with “Stuart Fails to Save the Universe,” the third offshoot of “The Big Bang Theory,” which forms a useful contrast with its predecessors. The straightforwardly titled “Young Sheldon,” a single-camera sitcom that ran for seven seasons on CBS before ending in 2024, wound back the clock to trace the childhood of Jim Parsons’ breakout character as a prodigy in Texas; “Georgie & Mandy’s First Marriage,” airing its third season this fall, follows Sheldon’s older brother in his time as a broke newlywed.
	

	Compared with such setups, which color within the lines of characters’ existing biographies, “Stuart Fails” takes a far more innovative approach. The HBO Max comedy — the first “Big Bang” spinoff to air on a streamer rather than on broadcast — does follow a figure from the juggernaut hit as he undertakes his own adventures. But Kevin Sussman’s titular comic book store owner doesn’t merely continue to tend to his small business in Pasadena, California. Instead, Stuart hopscotches through alternate dimensions in search of a way to undo a full-blown apocalypse. It’s a far cry from a few nerds trading one-liners in an apartment, and productively so.


	To pull off this hard genre pivot, “Big Bang” co-creators Chuck Lorre and Bill Prady have brought on sci-fi veteran Zak Penn (“X-Men: The Last Stand”) as a collaborator. “Big Bang” was always adjacent to science, of course; its protagonists were physicists and biologists in the professional orbit of Caltech, even if the show itself was more easy viewing than intellectually rigorous. But science fiction is new terrain, and from its first moments, “Stuart Fails” takes infectious delight in sticking Stuart and “Big Bang” geologist Bert (Brian Posehn) in the middle of a “Fallout”-style hellscape, complete with steampunk outfits, zombies and giant flying moths. You see, a couple members of the original “Big Bang” crew accidentally triggered the end of the world through some quantum something-or-other, and an alternate version of Stuart beams in from another timeline to say he alone can fix it.
	

	Stuart and Bert then secure the show’s driving MacGuffin, a machine that beams them into a new reality each episode, from “Big Bang” regular Kripke (John Ross Bowie) — here sporting the same Elmer Fudd-esque speech affect, but with a new day job as a bloodthirsty warlord. Stuart’s ex Denise (Lauren Lapkus) completes the central quartet, who serve as a grounding baseline while their surrounding environs vary wildly around them. One moment, they’re in a utopian society with no concept of private property; the next, they’re in a world where everything is basically normal, except people evolved from cows so they eat grass and chew cud. Sometimes the gang are magicians. Sometimes they’re centaurs.

	The playful, clever tone of “Stuart Fails” is underscored by a Danny Elfman theme song and a credits sequence that constantly swaps in new, jokey title cards to discourage skipping. (Plus, a literal “do not skip” sign, with an arrow pointing directly at the button.) Run times as short as 15 minutes maintain a breezy feel, and the distance of “Stuart Fails” from the original concept of “Big Bang” ironically makes it easier for familiar faces to drop in. I’m forbidden from disclosing which “Big Bang” stars outside the main cast of “Stuart Fails” make an appearance, but those who do can stop by for as few or as many cameos as they want, often while playing completely different people. One timeline’s scientist, it turns out, can be another’s horned demon. Of course, the constant change endemic to “Stuart Fails” means sacrificing some of the comforting sameness of a “Big Bang”-style hangout. But the comic chemistry among the main players provides just enough — and besides, the thrilling potential of a constantly wiped slate is worth the trade.

schema.headline: ‘Big Bang Theory’ Spinoff ‘Stuart Fails to Save the Universe’ Is a Playful, Creative Romp: TV Review

title: Jimmy Kimmel Thanks CBS for His Ratings Gains, Invites Stephen Colbert to Guest Host and Previews This Summer’s ‘Who Wants to Be a Millionaire’ (EXCLUSIVE)
description: There’s no certain proof, but it appears that the cancellation of CBS’ “The Late Show With Stephen Colbert” has given an early boost to ABC’s “Jimmy
body:
There’s no certain proof, but it appears that the cancellation of CBS’ “The Late Show With Stephen Colbert” has given an early boost to ABC’s “Jimmy Kimmel Live.” Before Kimmel went on his regular summer sabbatical, he pulled off a record-breaking June: The show averaged 3.15 million viewers, making it the show’s best-ever monthly audience, ever. Over 23 years of “Jimmy Kimmel Live!”

	“The ratings are very strong, which we are very grateful to CBS for their wisdom,” Kimmel slyly told Variety. “We were doing pretty well before that, but that really helped.”
	

	Does this mean orphaned Colbert fans have found a new home? “I’ve always thought of our show as an orphanage,” he quipped.


	“Jimmy Kimmel Live” was up 17% from May in viewers (2.69 million) and up 109% in adults 18 to 49 vs. the previous month (0.37 rating vs. 0.18 rating). That made for the best performance in the demo in six years (since April 2020).

	Overall, this was quite a season for “Jimmy Kimmel Live” — don’t forget, the entire mess of a controversy that saw him suspended for a few days was back in September. For the full TV season, “Jimmy Kimmel Live” was up 34% from last season (2.62 million vs. 1.95 million) and up 32% in adults 18 to 49 (0.22 rating vs. 0.17 rating). It was the most-watched season for the show since 2014-2015.

	It’s a reminder that even though viewership has shifted to YouTube and other social platforms, there’s still a sizable audience watching late night on linear. “It’s silly how people go wild about a podcast that’s No. 5, and the numbers still don’t even compare to a television show,” Kimmel says. “There’s still a lot of people watching late night television, and I include the other shows in that. And there are still a lot of people looking at our shows on YouTube the next day, and there’s a lot of people who are interested in what we have to say. They may just may not necessarily be watching it on their TV, but it seems like a little bit of people are coming back to their old friend television.”
	

	As Kimmel is off on his summer break, “Jimmy Kimmel Live” has returned to its rotation of guest hosts, including Tiffany Haddish, Anthony Anderson, Ike Barinholtz, Colman Domingo, Rosie O’Donnell and Jelly Roll. And yes, Kimmel told Variety that he asked Colbert if he’d like to guest host — but Colbert politely declined.

	“Of course, I asked him, and I think it feels premature,” Kimmel said. “But I told him, ‘anytime you want to host, it doesn’t matter if it’s the summer, I’ll gladly step aside.”

	Meanwhile, while it may be summer break for Kimmel in late night, he returns to primetime on Wednesday, July 22, at 8 p.m. ET on ABC for this year’s new celebrity-fueled edition of “Who Wants to Be a Millionaire.”

	“I like doing it, it’s a lot easier than my show, and the drama makes it fun,” Kimmel said. “It really feels like batting practice, somebody’s throwing balls and you’re just swinging. You don’t have to sit there and figure out what you’re going to do, what you’re going to say. There’s no writing involved, really. It’s nice to just be there, relying on your wits. People can watch as a community, you can watch it with your kids.”

	Continuing the trend from recent seasons, celebrities are paired up as they try to win money for charity.

	“I think people are more decisive when they’re playing alone,” Kimmel said. “Because there’s no one to talk to, and there’s no one to discuss with. I think they take more time when it’s a duo, for sure. 



	

					Ben Affleck joins game show champion Jamie Ding (Disney/Eric McCandless)
									Disney
	



	This year’s season opens with Ben Affleck, paired with “Jeopardy!” champion Jamie Ding. It’s a bit of a sequel to last year’s opener, which paired Matt Damon with “Jeopardy” winner and host Ken Jennings.

	“I’m not sure Ben knew that he was paired with a champion when he agreed to do the show,” Kimmel said. “But when I told him, he seemed surprised and delighted. I think he was prepared to do it on his own. He’s very smart, that Ben Affleck.”

	Also on the first episode, Larry David and daughter Cazzie David play the game. 

	“Larry David was every bit as Larry David as you would hope he would be,” Kimmel said. “He showed up questioning why he was there, trying to figure that out. He was perturbed at every question. But I think he had fun. He played with his daughter, Cazzie, and she was nervous, and that’s always hard when it’s a family team, because you tend to know the same stuff. I felt like I was on an episode of ‘Curb Your Enthusiasm,’ which is how I feel every single time I’m with Larry.”
	



	

					Larry David teams up with his daughter, Cazzie David (Disney/Eric McCandless)
									Disney
	



	Here are a few more contestants this season, and Kimmel’s thoughts:

	Colin Jost & Michael Che: “I don’t really know those guys. I learned that Michael is very decisive, and Colin is not, and in a battle of between the decisive and indecisive, the indecisive always wins. Colin was really weighing things out. In general on ‘Millionaire,’ whatever your first thought is is probably the right answer unless you really have no idea. But people have a tendency to blurt out what they think is the answer, and then they take a long time talking themselves out of that correct answer, and usually they’ll come back to the correct answer, but sometimes they talk themselves out of it.”

	Michael Keaton & Jon Lovett: “They met on the set. We asked Michael Keaton to do it, and he said yes. And then we asked, who do you want your partner to be, and he listens to ‘Pod Save America.’ So he picked John Lovett, and, and they had fantastic chemistry. Even though they didn’t know each other, I think John is friends with Michael’s son, so there was a connection there.”

	Will Forte & Taran Killam: “Hilarious together, really funny together.”

	Brad Paisley & Clayton Kershaw: “A fun duo. Clayton Kershaw did well. Sometimes you worry with the athletes, but he was great.”

	Paul Scheer & Rob Huebel: “Very smart and very funny ont heshow.”

	Tig Notaro & Pete Holmes: “She pretends to be less smart than she actually is, and it made for a very funny dynamic between them.”

	Jason Alexander & Wayne Knight: “Whenever you see people who spent a lot of time together and who have a lot of shared experiences, it’s fun. It’s also just kind of fun to see them together, especially if you watched ‘Seinfeld.’ It always reminds me of the episode of ‘Cheers’ where Cliff went on ‘Jeopardy.'”

	Timothy Simons & Jackie Tohn: “It’s best when they argue.”

	Billy Porter & Leslie Odom Jr.: “Billy was Leslie’s teacher in musical theater school, and so that was interesting, that dynamic between them. Like a mentor/mentee situation.”

	Jesse Eisenberg & Paul Giamatti: “We ask them beforehand what their areas of expertise are, just to give us something to talk about. Jesse Eisenberg and Paul Giamatti have deep areas of knowledge. They are built for this game, those two, and they were great together. They just made a movie together, so they were really in the pocket as far as chemistry goes.”
	

	Sarah Jane Nader & Brooks Nader: “Not related to Ralph.”

	Other pairings include Michael Kosta & Wendi McLendon-Covey, Awkwafina & Paul Feig, and Krysten Ritter & Sarah Shahi.

	“Who Wants To Be A Millionaire,” from Sony Pictures Television’s Embassy Row and Kimmelot, is executive produced by Michael Davies and Jimmy Kimmel. The series is produced by Sony Pictures Television’s Embassy Row and Kimmelot.

schema.headline: Jimmy Kimmel Thanks CBS for His Ratings Gains, Invites Stephen Colbert to Guest Host and Previews This Summer’s ‘Who Wants to Be a Millionaire’ (EXCLUSIVE)

title: AI YouTuber Gossip Goblin Plans to Release AI Film ‘Gods Don’t Give Gifts’ in Movie Theaters
description: There’s no shortage of places to watch an AI-generated content, from TikTok to YouTube. But now “Gods Don’t Give Gifts,” a collection of four anthol
body:
There’s no shortage of places to watch an AI-generated content, from TikTok to YouTube. But now “Gods Don’t Give Gifts,” a collection of four anthology shorts, is set to screen in movie theaters for a limited release in October as the latest project from Gossip Goblin, the moniker of the buzzy AI-focused YouTuber Zack London. The film, which will screen Oct. 30, traces the fallout of humanity besting earthly limitations like death and memory through technology and how it grapples with humanity as a result.
	

	The film’s team includes London, who wrote and directed the project, and Fable, the AI studio backed by Amazon’s Alexa Fund investment arm and run by CEO Edward Saatchi. The team behind the project is working with a distributor (which Saatchi would not name) and is targeting an initial run in cities including New York, Los Angeles, San Francisco and Chicago, with the hopes of leveraging London’s 1.4 million YouTube subscribers to bolster the film’s screen count.


	The film industry has been seemingly revolutionized by the wave of projects from YouTube personalities-turned-filmmakers Curry Barker and Kane Parsons, with their respective films “Obsession” and “Backrooms” grossing more than $800 million globally so far. London aims to test whether online audiences will still head to a multiplex not only for a traditional movie, but one made entirely using AI.

	London told Variety he viewed the theatrical release as an “experiment,“ one that showcases the work he’s done with AI online for a theatrical audience.

	“I see it as like a linear path of like, ‘OK, prove that you can tell stories with AI,’” London said. “It’s not about fighting Hollywood or whatever. This is just a new medium to experiment with that bypasses a lot of the traditional barriers or obstacles or just like budgetary constraints.”
	

	That also means factors that traditionally signal a film‘s theatrical success — such as, say, box office gross — aren’t their barometers for whether the experiment is a success, Saatchi said.

	“We’re not trying to have some massive financial windfall here,” he said. “This is about just psychologically saying, ‘No. This is a work of art, works of art play in theaters, and we’re gonna we’re gonna help you if you’re a fan of this world, which there are many in America, to be able to see it on a big screen.’”

	London also believes the sentiment in AI has shifted across the industry, and those initially opposed to it can recognize its potential in getting projects one hard to green-light off the ground.

	“I don’t want to come off as being like I’m here to disrupt the industry, but more like, I don’t think this would ever get made any other way,” he said. “This would not get greenlit. It would be too expensive, and it would be too weird, and it would be too out there, and not palatable enough to justify how expensive it would be to make something of this caliber. So I think it’s enough to just get it out there as a personal milestone, and also I think it’s a benchmark of here’s what we can do. We’ve planted a flag in the ground, we got at theaters. If it is outright rejected and people burn down the theaters, cool. I don’t think that’s going to happen.”

	London and Fable are modeling the theatrical release on “Iron Lung,” YouTuber Markiplier’s film released earlier this year that ultimately grossed $51 million on a $3 million budget from more than 4,000 screens. It’s unclear which theaters will screen the films — and a representative for Cinema United, the trade group that represents major exhibitors including AMC and Regal, did not return a request for comment — but Saatchi said exhibitors were so far “extremely excited” by the thought of playing the film.

	“The AI side is important of course and makes people nervous, but the YouTube side is giving people that extra confidence to try – beyond the fact that it’s a great film” he said.

	Watch a 15-minute clip of the film here:

schema.headline: AI YouTuber Gossip Goblin Plans to Release AI Film ‘Gods Don’t Give Gifts’ in Movie Theaters

title: ‘Days of Thunder 2’: Jonathan Levine in Talks to Direct Tom Cruise in Paramount Sequel
description: Paramount’s “Days of Thunder” sequel is shaping up with Jonathan Levine (“50/50,” “Warm Bodies”) in negotiations to direct returning star Tom Cruise
body:
Paramount’s “Days of Thunder” sequel is shaping up with Jonathan Levine (“50/50,” “Warm Bodies”) in negotiations to direct returning star Tom Cruise.

	Cruise will also produce the film alongside Jerry Bruckheimer, who produced the original 1990 racing drama. Will Staples wrote the script.  The original film was directed by the late Tony Scott and followed Cruise as Cole Trickle, a scrappy race car driver who gets recruited to a new NASCAR team by a retired racing legend, played by  Robert Duvall. Cruise met Nicole Kidman, to whom he was married for 11 years, on the set of “Days of Thunder.”
	

	News broke in November 2024 that Cruise was in talks to reprise his role. This isn’t the first time Cruise has reprised one of his older roles, as he returned to the “Top Gun” (1986) world with the sequel “Top Gun: Maverick,” which released in 2022. That film was a massive success for Paramount, grossing just shy of $1.5 billion at the global box office. It also earned six Oscar nominations, winning for best sound. 


	In addition to his varied film work helming comedies like “Long Shot” and “The Night Before,” Levine recently directed episodes of “Nine Perfect Strangers” starring Nicole Kidman. Next up, Cruise is set to star in Alejandro G. Iñárritu’s dark comedy “Digger,” releasing from Warner Bros. on Oct. 2. 

	Paramount is due to release Levine’s next film, “Mr. Irrelevant” on Christmas Day. David Corenswet stars in the film, which follows the true story of John Tuggle, the “Mr. Irrelevant” of the 1983 draft, the last player chosen in the last round, by the New York Giants, and his enduring impact on his team and teammates. Levine filmography also includes “Warm Bodies” and “Longshot.”
	

	The Hollywood Reporter first reported the development update.

	More to come…

schema.headline: ‘Days of Thunder 2’: Jonathan Levine in Talks to Direct Tom Cruise in Paramount Sequel

title: Georgia City's 22-Year-Old Mayor Under Investigation For Alleged Spending Habits
description:
22-Year-Old Mayor in Georgia
                          Stripped of City Credit Cards In Probe Ove
body:
22-Year-Old Mayor in Georgia
                          Stripped of City Credit Cards In Probe Over Alleged Spending Habits
                      
                  
                                                                        Published 
                              July 22, 2026
                            9:11 AM PDT
                                                                            
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
            A young mayor in Georgia is being investigated for his alleged spending habits ... and the city he was elected to govern took away his city-issued credit cards while the probe plays out.
      
    
            22-year-old Jayden Williams was sworn in as mayor of Stockbridge just six months ago ... but according to Atlanta News First, lawmakers passed a resolution last week restricting his access to non-public areas, his use of city vehicles, and his use of city funds through his city-issued credit card.
      
    
            Williams was ordered to hand over his credit card and stop using cars owned by the city ... Williams had a Monday deadline, but mayor pro term Elton Alexander said he didn't know if Williams complied. Still, city officials claim they can deactivate the credit card remotely.
      

                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
            Alexander said the city council received "information" last week that raised red flags about Williams' spending. It's unclear what Williams was allegedly spending money on, or how much money is involved here.
      
    
            Williams has not been charged with a crime as of Wednesday ... and the investigation remains ongoing. As the probe plays out, Williams can still preside over council meetings and continue with his other mayoral duties.
      
    
            In a statement, Williams' team says ... "Mayor Williams welcomes a full and independent investigation and looks forward to the opportunity to address any allegations through the proper process."

title: Driver Fleeing Cops Dives Off Bridge In Failed Getaway Attempt in Missouri
description:
Police Pursuit In Missouri
                          Driver Hops Out of Moving Car, Dives Off Bri
body:
Police Pursuit In Missouri
                          Driver Hops Out of Moving Car, Dives Off Bridge
                      
                  
                                                                        Published 
                              July 22, 2026
                            8:34 AM PDT
                                                                            
      
    
        
                    
                  
      
    
            A Missouri man jumped ship during a police chase ... exiting his moving car and diving off a bridge into the lake below ... and it's all on video.
      
    
            Gavin Thor Lundgren -- a 26-year-old man from Kimberling City -- was arrested Saturday after police say he refused to comply with a routine traffic stop and led them on a wild pursuit that saw him dart across a two-lane bridge and dive into Table Rock Lake.
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
            Dashcam footage shows the driver getting out of his car and swan-diving.
      

            His form would probably get him a 10 at the Summer Olympics in Los Angeles in a couple years ... but, in this case, all it got him was a few extra minutes of freedom -- cops arrested the dude not long after this.
      
    
            Lundgren was booked into jail on charges of resisting arrest by fleeing, careless driving, third-degree assault, and interfering with an arrest, NBC News reports. KYTV -- a local outlet based in Springfield -- reports Lundgren allegedly headbutted a pair of cops.
      
    
            Even though he cleared one hurdle on the jump, it looks like the legal system is gonna put a whole lot more in front of him.

schema.headline: Driver Fleeing Cops Dives Off Bridge In Failed Getaway Attempt in Missouri

title: 'Mad Max 2: The Road Warrior' Star Mike Preston Dead at 93
description:
'Mad Max 2' Star Mike Preston
                          Reportedly Dead At 93
body:
'Mad Max 2' Star Mike Preston
                          Reportedly Dead At 93
                      
                  
                                                                        Published 
                              July 22, 2026
                            5:36 AM PDT
                                                                            
              
    
                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
            Actor Mike Preston -- best known for his iconic role as Pappagallo in "Mad Max 2: The Road Warrior" -- has died.
      
    
            Preston passed away on June 8 in Florida, according to Variety, citing his wife, Josie. His cause of death was not disclosed.
      
    
            Born in London, England in 1938, Preston launched his TV and movie career with the 1969 flick, “Stoney.”
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
            Years later, Preston scored his biggest role ever in the 1981 classic film, "The Road Warrior," starring Mel Gibson. Preston played Pappagallo, the charismatic leader of a settler group trying to bring peace to the violent wasteland. 
      

            Then, in 1983, Preston secured the role of Tim Thomerson in the sci-fi movie “Metalstorm: The Destruction of Jared-Syn.”
      
    
            The actor also had success on the small screen, scoring the part of Alec Shaw in 8 episodes of the 1984 TV series "Hot Pursuit."
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
            During the '80s and '90s, Preston also made cameos in other popular TV shows, such as “Ellen,” “Scarecrow and Mrs. King,” “Shadow Chasers,” “Outlaws,” “Superbly,” “Alien Nation,” “Dark Justice,” “Highlander,” “Steel” and “Law & Order.”
      
    
            And get this ... you can also hear Preston's voice in the 2002 video game, “The Getaway."
      
    
            Preston was 93 and is survived by his wife Josie and their two daughters.
      
    
            RIP

schema.headline: 'Mad Max 2: The Road Warrior' Star Mike Preston Dead at 93

title: Model Recruiter With Ties to Jeffrey Epstein Found Dead in France
description:
Jeffrey Epstein
                          Model Recruiter Who Introduced Young Women to Pedo Foun
body:
Jeffrey Epstein
                          Model Recruiter Who Introduced Young Women to Pedo Found Dead
                      
                  
                                                                        Published 
                              July 22, 2026
                            6:58 AM PDT
                                                                      |
                                                                Updated 
                                  July 22, 2026
                                7:43 AM PDT
                                    
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
            Daniel Siad -- a French professional model recruiter who arranged for scores of young women to meet Jeffrey Epstein -- is dead.
      
    
            Siad was found dead in his home near Paris on Monday ... a deputy public prosecutor at the Nanterre Judicial Court in France told CNN. The cause and manner of death are unclear.
      
    
            Epstein and Siad were friends and business associates for more than a decade ... during which time, Siad admitted he arranged for Epstein to meet with multiple female models.
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                                  
      
    
            Siad continued associating with Epstein after Epstein's 2008 conviction on sex crimes ... claiming Epstein assured him he was a changed man. He said he believed Epstein was a casting director for Victoria's Secret and a top modeling agency.
      

            He told CNN, "I trusted him and believed this guy was a professional person ... I never heard anything from anyone I introduced to him who came back to me that they had a bad situation."
      
    
      
        
              
    
    
            Two women who claim Siad introduced them to Epstein told CNN Epstein abused them.
      
    
            Siad's name appears more than 2,000 times in the Epstein Files. He sent emails in which he included pictures of models who could potentially serve as Epstein's assistants ... including one who Epstein responded was "Too old."
      
    
            Siad was facing his own legal issues before his death ... including a rape allegation that dated back to 1990.

schema.headline: Model Recruiter With Ties to Jeffrey Epstein Found Dead in France
